series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriations requests by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to
archival review and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description: Complaints and other unsolicited feedback received by an agency from the public concerning the agency and records pertaining to the resolution of complaints.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of item
number 1.1.048.

series_description: Incoming/outgoing and internal leadership, supervisory, managerial, executive, administrative, substantive, and other high-level correspondence in any media, including electronic communication, pertaining to the formulation, planning, implementation, interpretation, modification, or redefinition of the programs, services, or projects of an agency and the administrative regulations, policies, and procedures that govern them.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact
the State Archives when these
records have met their retention
periods. CAUTION: This records
series and item number 1.1.008
should be used only for
correspondence that is not
included in or directly related to
another records series on the
agency's approved schedule. For
example, a memorandum that
documents an appropriations
request must be retained for the
minimum retention period
prescribed by item number 1.1.004;
a letter concerning an audit for that
prescribed by item number 1.1.002,
etc.
SEE ALSO item number 1.1.011

series_description: Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence, in any media including electronic communication, pertaining to or arising from the routine operations of the policies, programs, services, or projects of an agency.
comments:
SEE comment to item number
1.1.007. SEE ALSO item number
1.1.010.

series_description: Any document that officially initiates, rescinds, or amends general office procedures.
Any document that officially initiates, rescinds, or amends a regulation, policy, or procedure that governs the programs, services, or projects of an agency.

Calendars, appointment books or programs and scheduling, or itinerary records, maintained by staff during business hours that document appointments, itineraries, and other activities of agency officials or employees.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact
the State Archives when these
records have met their retention
periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period.
See Open Records Decision 635
issued in December 1995 by the
Attorney General.

series_description: From agency legal counsel or the Attorney General, including any requests eliciting the opinions.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Opinions and
advice that set legal precedent or
exhibit historical value will be
evaluated by the Archives and
Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission for archival
preservation. Must

series_description: News, press releases, marketing files and collateral or any public relations files maintained or issued by an agency. Includes print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual records.
Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are excepted under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
legal_citation: Government Code), including records provided to the legislature under section 552.008.

series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are furnished to the public under Public Information Act (Chapter 552
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
legal_citation: Government Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.

series_description: Charts or diagrams that show the structure of an agency and the relationships and relative ranks of its departments and job positions.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be
transferred to the Archives and
Information Services Division,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Organizational charts
showing division/department level
detail are not considered archival

series_description: Plans and records relating to the process of planning new or redefined programs, services, or projects of an agency that are neither included in nor directly related to other records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description: Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency, and adopted rules; open meetings notices; or any other documents required by law to be submitted to the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description: Drafts of proposed legislation and related correspondence.
Surveys soliciting feedback from customers, clients, or stakeholders of an agency, and the statistical data maintained rating an agency's performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See item number 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys

series_title: Speeches, Papers and Presentations- Disaster Film/Presentation Letters
series_description: Notes or text of speeches, papers, presentations, or reports delivered in conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_description: Records created by or on behalf of an agency in anticipation of or in the adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation. See page ix
for more information.

series_description: Logs or similar records used to register persons appearing before state agencies as required by Chapter 2004
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
legal_citation: Government Code, including quarterly reports filed with the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).

series_description: Operational strategic plans, which include descriptions of information resources programs, prepared in accordance with
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
legal_citation: Government Code, 2054.095 and 2056.002. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating the final plan.

series_description: Self evaluations and plans documenting compliance with the requirements of the Americans With Disabilities Act.
Notes taken during open meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils from which written minutes are prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_description: Documents submitted at meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils, including exhibit items, documentation for agenda items, public comment forms, etc. Includes documents sent in advance of meetings for briefing purposes, some of which may not be submitted at an actual meeting.
comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_title: Staff Meeting Minutes and Notes- Executive Committee Of Science Faculty
series_description: Minutes or notes, and supporting documentation, taken at internal agency staff meetings.
comments: Historical Data.

series_description: Minutes or notes, and supporting documentation, taken at internal agency staff meetings.

series_title: Reports - Biennial or Annual Agency (Narrative)- Reports
series_description: Biennial narrative reports to the governor and legislature as required by an agency's enabling statutes, including annual narrative reports if they are required by statute. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating final report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_title: Agency Rules, Policies, and Procedures - Surveys Reports Mandated
series_description: Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar records distributed internally for the use of employees or externally to the public or those individuals or entities regulated by an agency that sets out the rules, policies, and procedures that govern an agency's programs, services, or projects. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating rules, policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies,
and procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description: Reports made to the Office of Attorney General (OAG) on an agency's Public Information Act activities, per
Transcripts and final decisions of hearings conducted as part of the regulatory process, and hearings on proposed rules and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records that
lead to substantial or statutory
changes to the operations of the
agency and its policies and
procedures should be retained for
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.

series_title: Sunset Review Report and Related Documentation
series_description: Sunset Review Report, agency self-study, and other correspondence and supporting documentation related to the Sunset review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
response to the commission's draft
report and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations. The final
version of the agency self-
evaluation report must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(1).

series_description: Final agreement described by
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code,
Chapter 154.071.; Government Code, 2009.054(c), associated with a matter conducted under an alternative dispute resolution procedure in which personnel of a state agency participated as a party on the agency's behalf.

series_description: Copyright records which pertain to employees’ original work. May include but not limited to a copy of the work itself as submitted to the United States Copyright Office and the corresponding copyright application, registration notice, and supplementary documents.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Master versions of all forms used internally and externally by the agency, including subsequent revisions to an agency form or any associated design or design modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR 105) that identifies the records that are created and maintained by an agency, provides the minimum timeframes the records must be retained, and includes instructions for their disposition. This series may include working files and documentation of certification and approval by the Texas State Library and Archives Commission.
comments:
Original is retained by the State
and Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description: Forms used to track the transmittal of records to/from onsite and offsite storage areas or a transfer of physical custody.
Logs or similar records listing records disposed of by an agency, which might include records series title, dates of records, and date of disposition.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description: Worksheets or working papers used to capture records inventory information, including location tracking and to document existence and requirements of a records series.
Documentation used to provide and improve access to records. Includes indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers, guides, and other finding aids.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must
carry the same retention period
and archival code of the records
they support.

series_description: Records management plans and similar records that establish the policies and procedures under which records and information are managed in an agency.
Transmittals and related service documentation (e.g. form RMD 109) for disaster recovery services provided by TSLAC or other entities.
Forms used by TSLAC (e.g. form RMD 113) or other entities to establish disaster recovery services, authorize agency staff to access the media, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: One copy of each state publication as defined on page xi of this schedule, except a publication that is subject to a different retention period in this schedule. For example, a meeting agenda (see item number 1.1.058) also meets the definition, but it must be retained permanently; item numbers 1.1.004, 1.1.055, 1.1.066, 1.1.068, and 4.5.003, which also meet the definition, are closely associated with the appropriations process and must be retained AC + 6.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
(Government Code, 441.103-
441.105). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will
retain a copy of all state
publications submitted to it on a
continuing basis, subject to
periodic evaluation to determine if
the publication merits further
retention.
For additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the internet, consult
the administrative rules of the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (13 TAC
§§3.1-3.8).

series_description: Relatively long-lived computer files containing organized and consistent sets of complete and accurate electronic records and associated processing files. Examples include, but are not limited to, data tables of relational databases used by applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
for re se ma be archi al
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of
AC to agency master files and
application data that the files do
not fall under records series listed
elsewhere.

series_description: Agency-developed automated software applications, operating system files, and associated processing files, including job control language, programs, applications, scripts, source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description: Records documenting operational and maintenance requirements of computer hardware such as operating manuals, hardware/operating system requirements, hardware configurations, equipment control systems, and associated processing files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description: Records adequate to specify all technical characteristics necessary for reading or processing electronic records; to document modifications to computer programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate, and interpret data in an automated system; and allow for their timely, authorized disposition. Records include but are not limited to: user guides, system or sub-system definitions, system specifications, input and output specifications, system flow charts, program flow charts, program maintenance logs, change notices, data element dictionaries, file layouts, code books or tables, and other records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure, logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_title: Chargeback Records to Data Processing Services Users
series_description: Records documenting usage, costs, billing, cost recovery, budgeting, and administrative functions of computer usage and data processing services for individual units/departments/divisions in an agency.

Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents establishing data processing policies and procedures in an agency in such areas as access and security, systems development, data retention and disposition, data ownership, production control, system back-up, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading
or processing of electronic records.
SEE item number 2.1.009.

series_description: Forms and logs used to enter and reconcile data sets submitted for processing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title: Software Registrations, Warranties, and License Agreements
series_description: Records documenting the registration and licensing of a software application to activate the software for legal use by the end users of a state agency, along with warranties providing that the software will perform in accordance with functional specifications.
Records documenting the request for and response to help desk tickets received by divisions or units, such as information technology.
Biennial information security plan for protecting the security of the agency’s information.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to the counseling of an employee for work- related, personal, or substance abuse problems. Usually maintained at the supervisorial level except in those agencies with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Internal or external announcements or advertisements of job openings, promotions, training programs, or opportunities for overtime.

Includes a contract or agreement regarding the terms and conditions of employment of an individual and a contract with a vendor for temporary staffing services. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description: Includes a contract or agreement regarding the terms and conditions of employment of an individual and a contract with a vendor for temporary staffing services. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description: Records relating to the review of employee grievances or complaints against personnel policies, working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the U.
S. Department of Labor. SEE item
number 1.1.048.

series_description: Job evaluations, performance appraisals, or other similar documents used to evaluate the performance of an employee.

Disciplinary actions are those actions which may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are imposed to discipline an employee whose conduct is harmful to the best interests of the state, the agency, or the employee work force; and for failure to improve performance or conduct following imposition of corrective action. May include cause for demotion, suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for failure to give written notice of resignation. May also be used to document evidence of employee self- improvement efforts, as well as favorable and unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Forms or similar records used to create or change information concerning the records of an employee including pay grade, position classification, employee number, evaluation date, and termination of employment.
Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill statements, for positions in an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_title: Criminal History Checks- Employee Consent Form
series_description: Criminal history record information on job applicants or agency employees obtained from the Department of Public Safety (DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description: Certificates of completion, certifications, licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar records documenting the training, testing, certification, licensing, or continuing education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Summary of apprenticeship applicant qualifications, evaluation basis for selection or rejection, original applications, job assignments, promotions, separations, layoffs, terminations, compensation records, conditions of work, time records, hours of training provided (separate from labor), minority status, and gender of all selected and rejected applicants.
Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title: Employee Drug Testing and Screening Records – Positive Results and Calibration
series_description: Records of employee alcohol test results indicating an alcohol concentration of 0.02 or greater; records of employee verified positive controlled substances test results; documentation of refusals to take required alcohol and/or controlled substances tests; employee evaluation and referrals; calibration documentation; records related to the administration of the alcohol and controlled substances testing programs; copy of each annual calendar year summary.
legal_citation:
49 CFR 382.403 for
commercial motor
vehicle drivers

series_title: Employee Drug Testing and Screening Records – Negative Results
series_description: Records of negative and canceled controlled substances test results and alcohol test results with a concentration of less than 0.02.

Agreements between employee and agency authorizing certain actions, including acknowledgement forms, telecommuting agreements, outside/secondary employment authorizations, or other documentation that show proof of receipt and awareness of and adherence to agency policies and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Employee or applicant requests for reasonable accommodation under the ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Polygraph examinations and results that are administered as a part of the employment selection process.
Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including garnishment or other court-ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Payroll records and registers documenting employee earnings, wages, and pay. This may include but is not limited to payroll input records, summary statements, payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and payroll history.

Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including garnishment or other court-ordered attachments. Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Used to make increases or decreases to employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or in the computation of taxes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Summary of Accruals and
Premiums for Staff.

series_description: Employer's copy of "Employee’s Withholding Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Records defining the wage or salary rate for each position in the agency expressed in dollars, grades, or step numbers.

series_title: Unemployment Compensation Records- UCI (Unemployment Compensation) Log
series_description: Records and documentation relating to unemployment compensation claims, including reimbursement of funds disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission (TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Forms used to deposit employee’s earnings into a specified personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Records documenting the amount of pension or deferred compensation earned by individual employees.
Affirmative action plans for regular employees and apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments: Must be kept at least 5 years.

series_description: Affirmative action plans for contractors and subcontractors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Employee benefit plans such as pension; life, health, and disability insurance; deferred compensation; etc., including amendments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description: Reports providing statistical information on labor force.
Records relating to review and monitoring of job classifications within an agency.
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules, rosters, or assignments.
Reports to the agency from TWC or its predecessor pertaining to employees.

Requests and authorizations for travel; participation in educational programs, workshops, or college classes; or for other work-related activities for which the expenses of the employee are defrayed or reimbursed.

series_title: Personnel Policies and Procedures (includes Radiation Safety Manual)
series_description: Any internally distributed manuals, guidelines, or similar records that define agency wide policies and procedures concerning the personnel of an agency.
Any documents detailing the procedural duties and responsibilities of agency positions on a position-by-position basis.

series_title: Agency Staffing Reports- Employee List By Title/Dept.
series_description: Any reports compiled by an agency on aspects of personnel staffing, including listings of all staff by program or name, staff hired during a month, detailed listings of employees within its organizational structure, position vacancies, analyses of turnover rates and seasonality of employment, etc.
Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and checklists required of job applicants or of current personnel to qualify for promotion or transfer, including validation records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different master test (different in
terms of either questions or
administration procedures) should
be retained for the period
indicated.

series_description: Completed aptitude, competency, or skills test papers and checklists of job applicants or of current personnel taking a test to qualify for promotion or transfer.

series_title: EEO Reports and Supporting Documentation- EEOC Charges
series_description: Includes documentation used to complete EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity) reports.
Reports, studies, aggregated or summary data, and similar documentation compiled to monitor and demonstrate compliance with the federal Equal Pay Act.
Diversity and recruitment plans and related workforce analyses.

Used to create employee leave balances, to transfer leave balances when an employee transfers positions, to adjust carry-over balances on August 31, to correct errors on leave accumulation, and to close out leave accounts on separated employees.
Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle and provides employee leave status information for each position.

Records documenting individual employee’s hours worked, including work schedules and documentation evidencing adherence to or deviation from normal hours for those employees working on fixed schedules.

series_title: Time Off and/or Sick Leave Requests
series_description: Requests and authorizations for vacation, compensatory, sick, Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and other types of authorized leave, and supporting documentation.
Records documenting number of hours transferred in and out of comprehensive sick leave pool.
comments:
Leave Of Absence Transaction Edit
for Audits

series_description: Claims; invoices; statements; copies of checks and purchase orders; expenditure authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance, purchase, general journal, expenditure, and special vouchers; and similar records that serve to document disbursements, including those documenting claims for and reimbursement to employees for travel and other expenses.
comments:
Medicaid/Medicare Audit
Requirements

series_description: Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing registers, account cards, deposit warrants, cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts books, cash transfers, daily cash reports, reconciliations, general journal vouchers, special vouchers, and similar records that serve to document money owed to or received by a state agency and its collection or receipt.
Periodic reports compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Yearly report compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).

Sufficient records to verify information on unclaimed property previously reported to the State Comptroller showing the name and last known address of the apparent owner of reportable unclaimed property, a brief description of the property, and the balance of each unclaimed account, if appropriate.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description: Records documenting the internal and external procedural requirements with respect to the accounting department of a state agency.
Records of accounts deemed uncollectible, including write-off authorizations and returned checks, warrants, and drafts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Documentation relating to the capital and fixed assets of a state agency, including equipment or property history cards containing data on initial cost, depreciation schedules or summaries used for capital outlay budgeting, and property sale, auction, or disposal records of agency owned equipment and property.
Records documenting requests for payment of a sum of money according to the terms of a policy or contract.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: This series documents state, federal, and other sponsored grant projects participated in or administered by state agencies. It includes grant authorization records, which provide evidence of the award of grants to or by agencies; grant/project financial or performance reports, which are periodic reports of financial activity and/or program performance related to grants received or made by agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Retention requirements
may vary depending on the specific
funding agency. Records in this
series may be subject to
Government Code 441.1855.
Agencies must ensure that records
are retained for the appropriate
retention period.

series_description: Non-awarded, denied, or unfunded grant applications and proposals.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Records documenting financial obligations of a state agency that are not payable within one year of the date of the balance sheet, including debentures, loans, deferred tax liabilities, bonds, and pension obligations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Signature cards or similar records establishing authority of an agency employee to initiate or authorize financial transactions on behalf of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Logs documenting requests and receipt of FTI, including any information created by the recipient that is derived from federal return or return information received from the IRS or obtained through a secondary source.
Indirect cost plan and supporting documentation created or maintained in the development of the plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Bank statements, credit card statements, cancelled checks, check registers, deposit slips, debit and credit notices, reconciliations, and other banking related records.
Required by the General Appropriations Act. Includes encumbrances and documentation about budget transfers and revisions, as well as detail charts of accounts.
Records documenting incoming or outgoing deliveries, including through private courier services.
Any mailing address, telephone or fax number, or e-mail address records maintained by an agency on its employees or on entities or persons it serves.
Records and reports of postage expenses, including postal meter usage.
Includes word processing and data processing.
Licenses and permits obtained from external agencies or organizations in order to perform operations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include licenses
and permits issued by an agency as
part of its statutory responsibilities.

series_title: Photocopier and Telefax Usage Logs & Reports
series_description: Registers or logs of print copies and fax transmissions made by user or in total.
Schedules of prices charged by an agency for services to the public or other agencies, including any documentation used to determine the charges.

Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description: Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description: Any internally distributed manual, guidelines, or similar records that establish standard office procedures for an agency, e.g. agency style manuals, telephone protocols, mail room procedures, print shop and photocopy ordering instructions.
Any record created by an agency to track any type of incoming and outgoing correspondence or packages by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) or by private couriers.
List of agency contracts, leases, and agreements including general obligation, land lease, utilities, and construction contracts.
Assessments or evaluations of the value of state-owned buildings or property.

Records documenting building space requests and approvals. Information on request forms may include but is not limited to: the building and room; the reason for the change; parties involved in the request; date of request and approval.
Records documenting the determination, checking, or rectifying of any instrument giving quantitative measurements.
comments:
CAUTION: Some equipment and
instruments may require longer
retention period. State agencies
must determine if longer retention
period is required based on the
type of equipment or instruments
they use within their agency.
legal_citation:
Per Texas Regulation
for Control of
Radiation, Part 32

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title: Estimate Files (Supply and Repair Cost Estimates)
series_description: Quotes for minor construction and repair projects and supplies. Includes supporting documentation, as applicable.
Requests or work orders for repairs or maintenance to facilities, vehicles, or equipment completed by internal agency personnel or external service providers.
Any type of usage report or log used to monitor utilities such as gas, electric, water, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include utility
usage reports for agencies that
operate their own utilities.

series_description: Equipment and material descriptions and specifications that may include but are not limited to detailed descriptions; lists of raw materials and ingredients; physical characteristics of items; special handling procedures; or technical drawings.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Reservation logs or similar records relating to the use of agency facilities such as meeting rooms, auditoriums, etc.
Reports summarizing efficiency of facility space utilization, which may include data on room usage, demand, allocation, and capacity.
Documentation of mail returned by the postal or other mail delivery services as undeliverable.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: If corrected address
provided and mail rerouted,
maintain address update
documentation for prescribed
retention period.

series_description: Freight or cargo claims against carrier for damage or loss to a shipment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, awarded and unawarded bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on our after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
SEE related item number 5.1.001
Contracts and Leases and 5.2.028
Building Construction Contract and
Inspection Records.

series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, awarded and unawarded bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
comments:
SEE related item number 5.1.001
Contracts and Leases and 5.2.028
Building Construction Contract and
Inspection Records.
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description: Invalid bids that do not meet agency submission requirements and are not included in bid evaluation process (e.g. withdrawn, missed submission deadline, incomplete submission, etc.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
SEE related item number 5.1.001
Contracts and Leases and 5.2.028
Building Construction Contract and
Inspection Records.

series_description: Log, register, etc., providing a record of purchase orders issued, orders received, and similar data on procurement status.
W-9 IRS Form used to request a taxpayer identification number (TIN) for reporting information to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). This includes W-9 forms received by a state agency from vendors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Plans for evacuation of agency facilities in cases of emergency.

Fire, safety, and emergency drill, alarm, and other inspection records of agency facilities and equipment, including orders issued by inspectors to correct deficiencies in compliance with any code or regulations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
inspection reports of building
construction. SEE item number
5.2.028.

series_description: Plan that provides information to employees about the hazardous chemicals to which they may be exposed to in their workplace.
List of each hazardous chemical normally present in the workplace.
Logs, registers, or similar records documenting visitors to limited access or restricted areas of agency facilities.

Records relating to the issuance of keys, identification cards, building passes, passwords, signed statements or similar instruments of access to agency or state facilities, equipment or automated systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Reports of accidents to adults on state property or in any other situation in which the state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Reports of accidents to minors on state property or in any other situation in which the state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Environmental, biological, and material safety monitoring reports, including health or physical examination reports or certificates of employees, who have experienced exposure to toxic substances, harmful physical agent, or bloodborne pathogens in the workplace, including analyses derived from such reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 30 years

series_description: Environmental, biological, and material safety monitoring reports, including health or physical examination reports or certificates of employees, for whom periodic monitoring of health or fitness is required concerning toxic substances, harmful physical agent, or bloodborne pathogens in the workplace, including analyses derived from such reports.
Documentation relating to disputed long distance calls, including documents evidencing repayment by employees for personal long distance use.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Includes mileage, fuel/oil consumption, passengers carried and other related operational information.
Vehicle titles, registration information, and owner manuals for state vehicles.
